channel.title: theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Single DMT dose treats stress-induced depression more effectively than Prozac in mice
item.description: Psychedelics are psychoactive substances that trigger unusual mental states, also referred to as "trips," altering the perceptions, thoughts, and emot...

item.title: Healthy versions of low-carb and low-fat diets linked to better cardiovascular and metabolic health
item.description: The quality of a low-carbohydrate or low-fat diet may matter more than the amount of carbohydrates or fat consumed when it comes to reducing heart dis...

item.title: A single oncolytic virus injection may help T cells infiltrate glioblastoma
item.description: A team led by investigators at Mass General Brigham and Dana-Farber Cancer Institute has shown that a single injection of an oncolytic virus—a genetic...

item.title: Fragile X study uncovers brainwave biomarker bridging humans and mice
item.description: Numerous potential treatments for neurological conditions, including autism spectrum disorders, have worked well in lab mice but then disappointed in ...

item.title: Key gene behind drug resistance in small cell lung cancer identified
item.description: A research team has discovered a crucial mechanism that underlies chemotherapy resistance and metastasis in small cell lung cancer (SCLC). Their study...

item.title: RHR: Vitamin D: Why a Personalized Approach Is Best
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris Kresser explains why vitamin D supplementation should never be one-size-fits-all. Drawing on new res...

item.title: RHR: Cold Exposure: Why Sustained Beats Extreme
item.description: Cold plunges and ice baths dominate social media, but do they really deliver the best metabolic benefits? In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, ...

item.title: RHR: Why Your Sleep Schedule Matters As Much As Duration

item.title: RHR: Menopause Brain Fog—What’s Really Happening (and How To Fix It)
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris examines research confirming that menopause-related brain fog is real, measurable, and reversible. H...

item.title: RHR: Seed Oils and Colon Cancer – What the New Research Really Shows
item.description: Seed oils have become one of the most controversial topics in nutrition, especially after a 2024 University of South Florida study linked high omega-6...
